_note: REFERENCE SPEC, NOT a runnable config. Checkly's real config is JS/TS (checkly.config.ts) and Better Stack uses its own dashboard/Terraform. This is a plain-JSON description of the two synthetic checks the owner should create, written so it can be hand-translated into either tool (or imported by a small script). Nothing here is auto-loaded by the CRM or CI. The dependency-free GitHub Actions workflow at .github/workflows/uptime.yml already gives baseline 10-min uptime alerting for free; adopt this spec when you want multi-region probing + a public status page.
_provider_hint: Recommended: Checkly (API checks + status pages) or Better Stack (Uptime + status page). Both support: scheduled API checks, multi-region, response-status assertions, and a hosted status page.

project.name: DestinOraclesSolution CRM
project._comment: logicalId is Checkly's stable identifier for the project; keep it constant across edits so checks update in place instead of duplicating.

defaults._comment_frequency: Probe every 5 minutes. GitHub Actions cron can drift / coalesce under load; a dedicated monitoring vendor gives tighter, more reliable scheduling.
defaults._comment_locations: Multi-region so a single-region network blip does not page you. ap-southeast-1 (Singapore) is closest to the Malaysia user base and is the most representative of real latency; us-east-1 + eu-west-1 catch global edge issues. A real outage shows red from ALL regions.

checks._comment: Check 1 — homepage availability. Plain GET, expect 200.
checks.name: CRM homepage 200

assertions._comment: Fail the check if the homepage is anything other than 200.

checks._comment: Check 2 — Supabase auth health probe. POST a dummy login; the GoTrue endpoint rejects bad creds with HTTP 400 BEFORE checking the password, so 400 = backend UP. A 521 (dead Cloudflare origin) or any 5xx = backend DOWN. This is the same semantics as .github/workflows/uptime.yml.
checks.name: Supabase auth up (expects 400)

headers._comment: Public publishable key — already shipped in the browser bundle, safe to commit. Do NOT put a service/secret key here.

assertions._comment: 400 = healthy (dummy creds correctly rejected by a live backend). 521/5xx = DOWN → this assertion fails and pages you. Note: 401/403/429 also fail this strict EQUALS — in a vendor, add a 'tolerate' rule or a second soft assertion to treat those as warn-not-page (throttling), matching the workflow's warn-but-pass branch.

alertChannels._comment: PLACEHOLDER — replace with your real channel before importing. Checkly/Better Stack support Slack, email, PagerDuty, Opsgenie, SMS, and generic webhooks. Reuse the same OPS_SLACK_WEBHOOK URL as the GitHub Actions workflow so all monitoring fans into one ops channel.

sendOn._comment: Page on failure AND recovery so the on-call knows when it clears; degraded = slow-but-up early warning.

escalation._comment: Page after a single failed run across regions. Raise to 2 if you see flapping from a noisy region.

statusPage._comment: PLACEHOLDER — a public status page so customers/staff can self-check during an incident instead of emailing support. Checkly Status Pages + Better Stack both host this for free on a *.checkly-status.com / *.betteruptime.com subdomain, or map your own (e.g. status.destinoraclessolution.com via CNAME).
statusPage.name: DestinOraclesSolution CRM Status
statusPage._url_comment: Map this CNAME to the vendor's status-page host once created.

_adoption_steps: 1. Pick a vendor (Checkly free tier covers this comfortably; Better Stack also fine).
2. Recreate the two checks above (homepage 200; auth probe expecting 400) at 5-min frequency in us-east-1 + eu-west-1 + ap-southeast-1.
3. Wire the alert channel to your real Slack/PagerDuty webhook (reuse OPS_SLACK_WEBHOOK).
4. Publish the status page and CNAME status.destinoraclessolution.com to it.
5. Optional: once the vendor probe is trusted, you can drop the schedule in .github/workflows/uptime.yml to manual/dispatch-only to avoid duplicate alerts — or keep both as belt-and-suspenders.
